comment_text.sn8.8:5.2: The idea of the seven seers, long proverbial in Vedic culture, was adapted for Gotama in DN 14, where he is the seventh recorded Buddha in the lineage starting with Vipassī. Various texts and commentaries also allow the sense, “best of seers”.
comment_text.sn8.8:5.4: In a cold northern climate this sounds unappealing, but in the heat the generous rain is a cool blessing.
comment_text.sn8.8:8.1: The “wrong path” (ummagga) is one that takes you to the wrong destination (MN 107:14.26).
comment_text.sn8.8:8.3: Here Vaṅgīsa turns to address the monks directly.
comment_text.sn8.8:8.4: For asitaṁ bhāgaso pavibhajaṁ, compare the similar phrase vibhatto bhāgaso mito, which refers to the neatly ordered sections of hell (MN 130:16.4) or the suburbs (Snp 2.7:23.2). Vaṅgīsa is referring to the Buddha’s skill in breaking down complex or obscure matters and presenting them systematically.
comment_text.sn8.8:9.3: For this couplet I follow Bodhi’s note in Connected Discourses.
comment_text.sn8.8:10.2: Ṭhiti is viññāṇaṭṭhiti (“planes of consciousness”), i.e. seven realms in which consciousness can be reborn (AN 7.44, DN 15:33.1, DN 33:1.8.11). This is a subtle allusion to the Buddha’s practice under his former teacher Āḷāra Kālāma, where he learned up to the highest “plane of consciousness”, namely the dimension of nothingness. In Snp 5.15:3.1 he speaks of how he understood that attainment and went beyond it. | Not to be confused with the four “grounds for consciousness”, i.e. the first four aggregates, as insight does not consist in going beyond these to consciousness (DN 33:1.11.122, SN 22.54:1.11).
comment_text.sn8.8:10.4: Vaṅgīsa continues to invoke the Buddha’s life story by alluding to the first teaching, Rolling Forth the Wheel of Dhamma (SN 56.11). After leaving behind those who taught rebirth in the “planes of consciousness”, the Buddha realized Awakening and proceeded to Varanasi to teach the group of five. This anticipates the next discourse, which features one of the five.

root_text.sn8.8:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ aḍḍhatelasehi bhikkhusatehi.
root_text.sn8.8:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā bhikkhū nibbānapaṭisaṁyuttāya dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.sn8.8:1.3: Te ca bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.sn8.8:1.5: “ayaṁ kho bhagavā bhikkhū nibbānapaṭisaṁyuttāya dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.sn8.8:1.6: Te ca bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.sn8.8:1.7: Yannūnāhaṁ bhagavantaṁ sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthaveyyan”ti.
root_text.sn8.8:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn8.8:2.4: Atha kho āyasmā vaṅgīso bhagavantaṁ sammukhā sāruppāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:
root_text.sn8.8:7.1: “Kiṁ nu te, vaṅgīsa, imā gāthāyo pubbe parivitakkitā, udāhu ṭhānasova taṁ paṭibhantī”ti?
root_text.sn8.8:7.2: “Na kho me, bhante, imā gāthāyo pubbe parivitakkitā, atha kho ṭhānasova maṁ paṭibhantī”ti.
root_text.sn8.8:7.3: “Tena hi taṁ, vaṅgīsa, bhiyyoso mattāya pubbe aparivitakkitā gāthāyo paṭibhantū”ti.
root_text.sn8.8:7.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā vaṅgīso bhagavato paṭissutvā bhiyyoso mattāya bhagavantaṁ pubbe aparivitakkitāhi gāthāhi abhitthavi:

translation_text.sn8.8:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery, together with a large Saṅgha of 1,250 mendicants.
translation_text.sn8.8:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk about extinguishment.
translation_text.sn8.8:1.3: And those mendicants were paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.sn8.8:1.5: “The Buddha is educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk about extinguishment.
translation_text.sn8.8:1.6: And those mendicants are paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.sn8.8:1.7: Why don’t I extoll him in his presence with fitting verses?”
translation_text.sn8.8:2.1: Then Venerable Vaṅgīsa got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.sn8.8:2.2: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.sn8.8:2.3: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.sn8.8:2.4: Then Vaṅgīsa extolled the Buddha in his presence with fitting verses:
translation_text.sn8.8:3.3: as he teaches the immaculate Dhamma,
translation_text.sn8.8:3.4: extinguishment, fearing nothing from any quarter.
translation_text.sn8.8:4.1: They listen to the immaculate Dhamma
translation_text.sn8.8:4.2: taught by the fully awakened Buddha;
translation_text.sn8.8:4.4: at the fore of the mendicant Saṅgha,
translation_text.sn8.8:5.3: You are like a great cloud
translation_text.sn8.8:6.2: out of desire to see the teacher.
translation_text.sn8.8:7.1: “Vaṅgīsa, had you previously composed these verses, or did they spring to mind in the moment?”
translation_text.sn8.8:7.2: “They sprang to mind in the moment, sir.”
translation_text.sn8.8:7.3: “Well then, Vaṅgīsa, speak some more spontaneously inspired verses.”
translation_text.sn8.8:7.4: “Yes, sir,” replied Vaṅgīsa. Then he extolled the Buddha with some more spontaneously inspired verses, not previously composed:
translation_text.sn8.8:8.3: See him, the liberator from bonds,
translation_text.sn8.8:8.4: dissecting the unattached section by section.
translation_text.sn8.8:9.1: You have explained in many ways
translation_text.sn8.8:9.2: the path to cross the flood.
translation_text.sn8.8:9.3: The Seers of Truth stand unfaltering
translation_text.sn8.8:9.4: in the freedom from death explained by you.
translation_text.sn8.8:10.1: As the bringer of light who has pierced the truth,
translation_text.sn8.8:10.2: you’ve seen what lies beyond all planes.
translation_text.sn8.8:10.3: When you saw and realized this for yourself,
translation_text.sn8.8:10.4: you taught it first to the group of five.
translation_text.sn8.8:11.1: When the Dhamma has been so well taught,
translation_text.sn8.8:11.2: how could those who know it be negligent?
translation_text.sn8.8:11.3: That’s why, being diligent, we should always train

variant_text.sn8.8:10.1: ativijjha → ativijjha dhammaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn8.8:11.2: pamādo vijānataṁ dhammaṁ → ko pamādo vijānataṁ (bj, sya-all, km)
